behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the target file, deprecated APIs (parser.ParseDir, doc.New), required replacements (parser.ParseFile, doc.NewFromFiles), required import (go/ast), and required unexported helper for directory parsing.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken and required APIs but doesn't give exact code or line numbers; agent must figure out how to implement parseDir.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_parse_dir_helper_exists requires function named exactly 'parseDir' with first parameter '*token.FileSet'; test_uses_doc_new_from_files requires literal variable name 'fset' as first argument to doc.NewFromFiles; alternative correct fixes using different names (e.g., parsePackageDir, different FileSet variable name) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests run go build, go test, go vet, gofmt subprocesses in addition to regex checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require actual compilation and test-pass, so a stub cannot satisfy the f2p checks.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 11 criteria in instruction.md are tested including build, vet, gofmt, test pass, and API-usage checks.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings labeling fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass categories.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code modification, no structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and commands in instruction match the repo paths and test expectations.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction identifies the file and deprecated APIs since that's the task scope, but doesn't give line-level fixes.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, not a config edit.
